Uses his CPAP machine every night at home and relies on nightly CPAP data downloads for ongoing sleep quality monitoring — no longer tracks sleep via the clinic’s equipment or general monitoring apps
Enters fatigue and sleep quality ratings each morning into the CPAP app’s daily log for digital sharing with his clinician; has discontinued the paper journal now that the formal sleep study is over
Performs a detailed nightly CPAP setup routine before bed, including checking mask fit and humidifier water level — does not skip even when tired or running late
Avoids evening naps or reclining in a recliner after dinner to ensure sleep pressure and maximize CPAP effectiveness at bedtime

superseded_preferences.fact: Schedules all team meetings before 8am or after 4pm — never during midday shift changes
superseded_preferences.reason: He is no longer managing warehouse shift teams or handling shift changes; his new task force role centers around corporate daytime meetings, making this preference irrelevant.

superseded_preferences.fact: Takes handwritten notes during troubleshooting sessions — never relies solely on digital logs
superseded_preferences.reason: Troubleshooting sessions themselves are now temporarily limited or avoided during migraine flare-ups, per neurologist's recommendation; note-taking becomes largely irrelevant when such sessions are restricted.

superseded_preferences.fact: Always answers urgent inventory system issues personally before escalating to IT
superseded_preferences.reason: With chronic migraines and a doctor requiring restricted screen time during flare-ups, he can no longer reliably answer urgent troubleshooting issues himself; he must now delegate these tasks to others until his symptoms abate.

superseded_preferences.fact: Researches supply chain management best practices online every Sunday morning for professional growth
superseded_preferences.reason: Now enrolled in a company-sponsored certification program, his Sunday mornings for professional growth shift from informal research to studying assigned material for the coursework and capstone, as formal requirements take priority.

superseded_preferences.fact: Packs a homemade low-sodium lunch every workday — never eats from the onsite vending machines
superseded_preferences.reason: The overseas assignment prevents him from preparing lunch at home and packing it for work as usual; he now must rely on meal options available near his temporary workplace while maintaining his health requirement.

superseded_preferences.fact: Goes for a brisk 20-minute walk during lunch breaks — avoids gym memberships due to unpredictable schedule
superseded_preferences.reason: With the new fixed 9-to-5 hours and reliable lunch break, he can now consistently use the onsite gym, changing his previous avoidance of gym memberships due to unpredictable shifts.

superseded_preferences.fact: Uses a sleep tracking app nightly to monitor sleep quality — avoids late caffeine to help with headaches
superseded_preferences.reason: With the sleep study underway and possible new recommendations or equipment coming from the clinic, he is instructed to discontinue consumer sleep apps and follow the clinic's monitoring protocols instead.

superseded_preferences.fact: Preps healthy snack containers (nuts, fruit) on Sundays to resist warehouse junk food during long shifts
superseded_preferences.reason: With the three-month overseas posting, his routine of prepping Sunday snack containers at home is impossible; he must adapt by sourcing healthy snacks locally.

superseded_preferences.fact: Blocks off Saturday mornings exclusively for helping his parents with errands — never schedules work during this time
superseded_preferences.reason: With his father's early-stage Parkinson's diagnosis, routine errands have expanded to include regular medical appointments and increased physical assistance. His caregiving responsibilities now require much more personal involvement for both logistical and emotional support, taking up a greater portion of his weekends.

superseded_preferences.fact: Sets aside Sunday evenings to review college essays and applications with his oldest child — keeps this as a regular commitment
superseded_preferences.reason: With his oldest child moving out-of-state to attend university, in-person essay review is replaced by remote check-ins to maintain their connection.

superseded_preferences.fact: Attends every school event at least once per semester for each child, even if it means shifting his work schedule
superseded_preferences.reason: The mandatory in-person 9-to-5 attendance policy removes his ability to shift work schedule for daytime school events, now restricting attendance to evenings or weekends.

superseded_preferences.fact: Plays strategy board games with family every Thursday night — never misses unless an emergency arises
superseded_preferences.reason: With his wife's mother temporarily moving into their home for post-stroke recovery, Thursday nights now require more caregiving and lower stimulation, making family board game sessions impractical for the time being.

superseded_preferences.fact: Goes fishing twice a month at a local reservoir with his brother — always wakes up before sunrise for these trips
superseded_preferences.reason: Care demands and need for household support mean he cannot commit to early morning fishing excursions while his wife's mother needs assistance.

superseded_preferences.fact: Works on model trains in the garage on rainy afternoons — never brings hobby projects into the main house
superseded_preferences.reason: Traveling for three months overseas makes working on physical model trains in his home garage impossible; he replaces this with portable, travel-friendly aspects of the hobby.

superseded_preferences.fact: Books one weekend getaway each season for the family — never travels without at least three weeks’ advance planning
superseded_preferences.reason: The temporary presence of his wife's recovering mother makes overnight trips impossible — family travel is not feasible during this caregiving period, so seasonal getaways cannot be planned.

superseded_preferences.fact: Joins the company gym for 30-minute indoor workouts during lunch breaks — takes advantage of the consistent 9-to-5 schedule
superseded_preferences.reason: Certification evening classes twice a week and capstone work means lunch breaks will be spent preparing or recovering for classes and overtime. His schedule is now more unpredictable and demanding, making regular gym visits impractical.

superseded_preferences.fact: Attends school events only if they are outside standard 9-to-5 work hours — unable to shift work schedule under new policy
superseded_preferences.reason: This preference was specifically tied to attending school events for his children. With his oldest now attending college out-of-state and soon leaving home, events for this child no longer occur in the local school context; only his younger child remains relevant for school events, so the preference needs redefining rather than persisting in its current form.

superseded_preferences.fact: Arrives at the distribution center at 8:45am every weekday to prepare for daily in-person coordination meetings before shift begins
superseded_preferences.reason: The leadership shakeup means he is reassigned from the warehouse to a new cross-departmental task force stationed at the corporate office, requiring him to coordinate with senior management in office settings rather than prep for team meetings on the warehouse floor.

superseded_preferences.fact: Sets a daily reminder to lock work files and tidy his desk at 5pm sharp before leaving — never stays late under the new shift rules
superseded_preferences.reason: With his new role involving irregular meeting schedules at the corporate office and no fixed shift end time, he must adapt his end-of-day routine to conference room transitions and variable departures, rather than following a strict 5pm rule.

superseded_preferences.fact: Begins organizing packing and travel checklists for the family's upcoming relocation weekends — dedicates Saturday afternoons for this
superseded_preferences.reason: The relocation weekends and travel-focused packing routines are no longer relevant—his weekends are now required at home and with his father due to new medical needs and regular appointments. There is no more actively planned family relocation at this time.

superseded_preferences.fact: Helps his wife prepare balanced meals and administer medication for her mother each evening — coordinates with his children to share caregiving duties
superseded_preferences.reason: He will be out of the country for three months and cannot physically assist with evening caregiving at home; these duties no longer apply during the assignment.

superseded_preferences.fact: Relies on overnight sleep clinic results and prescribed monitoring equipment to assess sleep quality — discontinues nightly sleep tracking app usage temporarily
superseded_preferences.reason: With an official diagnosis of moderate obstructive sleep apnea, his sleep monitoring now centers on CPAP machine data, not on clinic hardware or consumer sleep tracking apps. The clinic’s study phase has ended, and CPAP compliance and results become the core of his nightly routine.

superseded_preferences.fact: Keeps electronics (phone, tablet, TV) out of the bedroom after dinner on clinic's advice to prepare for more accurate sleep study results and reduce evening stimulation
superseded_preferences.reason: This restriction was specifically for improving the accuracy of the overnight sleep study. With the study completed and a CPAP machine now in use for nightly treatment, there is no longer a medical requirement to keep all electronics out of the bedroom for diagnosis purposes.

superseded_preferences.fact: Records detailed notes about fatigue and headache episodes in a paper journal each morning, as requested in the clinic's pre-study protocol
superseded_preferences.reason: The sleep study required paper journaling to capture symptoms for diagnosis. Now, ongoing CPAP management encourages digital self-reporting within the CPAP app for convenience and real-time sharing with his health provider.

superseded_preferences.fact: Dedicates both Saturday mornings and afternoons to assisting his father with household tasks, transportation, and medical appointments, rescheduling all nonessential activities and minimizing work interruptions on weekends
superseded_preferences.reason: During the three-month overseas assignment, he cannot provide in-person help to his father on weekends; local family responsibilities must be paused.

superseded_preferences.fact: Prepares a written log of his father's symptoms, medications, and appointments each weekend to streamline doctor visits and coordinate care with family members
superseded_preferences.reason: He is unable to attend his father’s appointments or maintain in-person logs from abroad during the assignment.

superseded_preferences.fact: Shifts weekend meal routines to include low-sodium, soft-texture dishes when dining with his parents, to accommodate his father’s dietary changes
superseded_preferences.reason: Since he will not be present for family meals during the overseas assignment, this meal routine does not apply.
